segments.text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Often, a story or a letter that we hear from the Scripture readings that's on the table,<|7.12|>
<|7.20|> so to speak, as we walk in on Sunday mornings, one of them can be very confusing, even jarring<|14.06|>
<|16.54|> And the Gospel reading today has some of that, so I'm not going to talk about it at all.<|22.44|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> because there are sometimes, just as often, readings that are actually just leap off the page,<|10.30|>
<|10.40|> and they're simple, and they're evocative, and they're rich, and they just stir the imagination.<|14.96|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And for me, that's what the epistle is today, in which Paul, and let me say, I'll just bracket and say, Paul, God bless him.<|8.70|>
<|8.70|> There are times where Paul, just intellectually, I wrestle and disagree with him.<|12.96|>
<|13.64|> But in this case, in this letter, the beginning of this letter, just immediately it resonates, and I know what he's talking about.<|20.74|>
<|20.82|> And I'm moved by it because he's writing to Timothy 2.<|24.70|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And he just simply says, Timothy, I remember your grandmother's faith, Lois.<|6.68|>
<|7.40|> I remember your mother's faith, Eunice.<|11.28|>
<|11.96|> And I know that your faith comes from them and has been passed down from those generations.<|18.98|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It's powerful. It just stirs my own imagination as I think about those elders and the faith in my own life.<|7.26|>
<|7.76|> This moment in Paul is really interesting because he's describing, and this would be, if you think about the New Testament, maybe the one path to faith.<|18.00|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.46|> There's a second path, but one path to faith already in the New Testament.<|4.08|>
<|4.26|> It's a smaller path, but it's families passing down the faith from one generation to the next.<|11.74|>
<|12.36|> Now, that's actually the smaller path in the New Testament because the church is in its infancy,<|17.46|>
